commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9fc8a097ccab39c2a3115b6341a6880222919442)
commands.review_summary:
The PR moves harness message types into `agent-core`, expands tsdown pre-cleaning to package dist outputs, preserves declaration files for skip-DTS builds, and hardens related tests.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests +99, Other +115. Total +219 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible: current main only cleans root tsdown outputs and still carries the session augmentation this PR removes. I did not run a build or tests because this review is read-only and must not create artifacts.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because current-head real behavior proof is missing and automation cannot supply the contributor's final-head proof for them.

target.title: fix(build): avoid stale agent-core dts warnings
target.body:
## Summary
- Move built-in harness message roles into the canonical agent-core message types so generated declarations no longer need relative module augmentation.
- Keep `src/agents/sessions/messages.ts` as a re-export while removing OpenClaw-only module augmentations.
- Make tsdown output-root handling generic across all tsdown-built packages and preserve existing declarations during skip-DTS clean builds.
- Harden the affected plugin-entrypoint test fixtures so they pass under stricter temp file mode checks.

## Validation
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/agent-core/src/harness/messages.test.ts test/scripts/tsdown-build.test.ts src/secrets/provider-integrations.test.ts src/secrets/resolve.manifest-registry.test.ts src/secrets/runtime.loadable-plugin-origins.test.ts src/secrets/configure.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/tsdown-build.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- Testbox through Crabbox targeted tests: `tbx_01ksx7ceb5vb3qh7533gkqv8b1`, run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26693464344, exit 0
- Testbox through Crabbox broad proof: `tbx_01ksx7ceb26jzps31t608zee8g`, run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26693464348, `check:changed` + `build`, exit 0
- GitHub CI: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26693461007, success

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: OpenClaw package builds should clean stale tsdown package output for every tsdown-built package without breaking skip-DTS builds that reuse existing declaration files; agent-core generated declarations should no longer rely on relative harness module augmentation for built-in harness messages.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through the OpenClaw Crabbox wrapper on PR head `9fc8a097ccab39c2a3115b6341a6880222919442`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/agent-core/src/harness/messages.test.ts src/secrets/provider-integrations.test.ts src/secrets/resolve.manifest-registry.test.ts src/secrets/runtime.loadable-plugin-origins.test.ts src/secrets/configure.test.ts test/scripts/tsdown-build.test.ts

git fetch --no-tags --depth=1000 https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw.git main:refs/remotes/origin/main
git merge-base origin/main HEAD >/dev/null || { git fetch --unshallow https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw.git || true; git merge-base origin/main HEAD >/dev/null; }
corepack pnpm check:changed
corepack pnpm build
```

Evidence after fix: Targeted Testbox lease `tbx_01ksx7ceb5vb3qh7533gkqv8b1` completed with exit 0. Broad Testbox lease `tbx_01ksx7ceb26jzps31t608zee8g` completed with exit 0. GitHub CI run `26693461007` completed successfully on the same head SHA.

Observed result after fix: Targeted touched tests, broad changed checks, full build, and GitHub CI completed with exit code 0/success. The skip-DTS cleaner now preserves existing declaration files recursively while removing stale JS output, covered by `test/scripts/tsdown-build.test.ts`.

What was not tested: No manual UI flow; this is build/type/test fixture behavior.
